bug_fix: .norm(-1) -> .norm(dim=-1): PyTorch .norm() first positional arg is p (norm order), not dim. .norm(-1) computes L_{-1} norm ≈ 0.0003, causing direction vectors to explode to ~11000. .norm(dim=-1) computes L2 norm along last dimension ≈ 6.0.

examples.baseline: The technology has improved in recent years, with a major breakthrough in Augmented Reality...
examples.nsep4: More recently, AI has evolved into a basic, generalized, and universal tool for solving complex problems...
examples.combo: Astonishingly, most of the recent advances in AI come from algorithms...

examples.baseline: Some areas of the field were given a boost by IBM...
examples.nsep4: In the same period, the field attracted the public as it was used in the development of training programs...
examples.npca32: Many major corporations saw an opportunity in a new market...

summary.norm_sep_advantage_confirmed: Pythia-410M: nsep4 beats naive4 by 1.7-3.3x consistently across context lengths
summary.long_context_stable: No degradation with longer prefill. ΔPPL decreases when baseline PPL is lower (longer, more predictable context).
summary.generation_quality: All methods produce coherent text with top-p sampling. No repetition (uniq=1.0). Greedy decoding repetition was a decoding issue, not compression.
summary.cv_high_but_expected: CV is high because baseline PPL varies dramatically with text content (45-107 for GPT-2). Absolute ΔPPL scales with baseline PPL.
